title: Why TypeScript?
description: Understand the massive benefits of static typing.

slides.title: The Bug Trap 🐛
slides.content:
In plain JavaScript, if you try to call a massive string method on a number variable, you won't know there's a problem until you *run the code in the browser*.

This leads to crashes in production and unhappy users.

slides.title: Early Detection 🛡️
slides.content:
TypeScript catches these bugs **before the code even runs!**

The compiler acts as a first line of defense. If you make a type mistake, `tsc` will block the compilation and throw an error, forcing you to fix it right immediately.

quizData.question: When does TypeScript catch type-related errors?

options.text: After the user clicks a button.
Before execution, during the compilation phase.

quizData.explanation: TypeScript catches errors at compile-time instead of run-time, leading to much more stable applications.

slides.title: World-Class Autocomplete ✨
slides.content:
Because TypeScript knows the shape of your data, code editors like VS Code can provide insanely helpful intelligent code completion (IntelliSense).

When you type a dot `.` after an object, TS instantly knows every valid property it has!

slides.title: Self-Documenting Code 📖
slides.content:
Type annotations act as documentation that is guaranteed to be up to date.

If you see a function `calculateTotal`, you don't need to guess if the parameters should be strings or numbers; the types tell you exactly what it needs!
